    Home › Ships › Disney Dream

    Disney Dream

    Disney Cruise Line · Cruise · In service · built by Meyer Werft

    Disney Dream led a new class in 2011 at around 130,000 GT and introduced the AquaDuck — the first water coaster ever fitted to a ship.

    Length overall340 m
    Beam37 m
    Gross tonnage129,690 GT
    Passengers4,000
    Speed22 kn
    Delivered2011
    Our numbers

    Derived metrics

    Computed by ships.fyi from the core specification — see how we calculate these.

    Passengers per metre#50 of 116
    11.8 pax/m passengers (double occupancy) ÷ length overall
    Length-to-beam ratio#3 of 142
    9.19 length overall ÷ beam
    Sense of scale

    How big is it, really?

    The Disney Dream's length overall, against things you already know the size of.

    Disney Dream340 m
    Eiffel Tower (height)330 m
    Titanic269.06 m
    Football pitch105 m
    Boeing 747-876.3 m
    Blue whale25 m
    You (probably)1.75 m

    The 747-8 held the longest-airliner record for over a decade, until Boeing's 777-9 took it by about 19 inches (76.72 m) — our sibling site aircraft.fyi measures everything that flies the same way.

    One Disney Dream = 1.26 Titanics · 3.2 football pitches · 14 blue whales · 4.5 Boeing 747-8s laid end to end.

    #45 of 142 by length → #75 by gross tonnage → #37 by passengers → Run the Canal-Fit Checker → Sailing on her? Get cruise-ready →

    Specification

    The numbers

    Length overall≈340 m
    Beam≈37 m
    Gross tonnage≈129,690 GT
    Passengers≈4,000 (max)
    Crew≈1,458
    Staterooms≈1,250
    Decks14 passenger decks
    Entered serviceJanuary 2011

    Compare it

    vs Disney Fantasy vs Disney Wish vs Disney Wonder Compare with anything →
    ← PreviousDisney Destiny Next →Disney Fantasy

    Last verified: 2026-07-15 · Spot an error? business@luck.fyi

    The story

    What makes it different

    Disney Dream ended a thirteen-year gap in Disney's shipbuilding and jumped the line's scale by more than 45,000 gross tons in a single step. She is also where Disney moved from Fincantieri to Meyer Werft, which has built every ship the line has ordered since.

    The AquaDuck is the reason people remember her. It is a 765-foot water coaster in a clear acrylic tube, propelled by water jets, that runs a circuit of the top deck and swings out over the side of the ship four decks above the promenade. No ship had carried one before, and the idea has since been copied widely.

    Her styling is Art Deco to Disney Fantasy's Art Nouveau, and both keep the line's deliberate ocean-liner profile: black hull, white superstructure, two red funnels with black tops. Only the aft funnel does any work — the forward one holds youth activity spaces, which is a very Disney solution to a naval architecture problem.

    The class

    The Disney Dream line

    Dream class
    Lead ship of two with Disney Fantasy (2012), and the first Disney ships built by Meyer Werft rather than Fincantieri.
    AquaDuck
    A 765-foot transparent-tube water coaster running around the top deck and cantilevered out over the ship's side — the first of its kind at sea.
    Operators

    Who operates the Disney Dream

    All 1 operator of record, verified 2026-07-15. Figures marked ≈ are approximate.

    DCLDisney Cruise LineShort Bahamas itineraries from Florida.1✓→
    Quick answers

    Disney Dream, asked and answered

    How long is the Disney Dream?
    340 m (1,115 ft) length overall — the #45 longest of the 142 ships on ships.fyi, and 3.2 football pitches end to end.
    How big is the Disney Dream compared to the Titanic?
    The Disney Dream is 1.26× the Titanic's length — 340 m against her 269.06 m. By volume the gap is starker still: 129,690 GT against Titanic's 46,328 GRT — roughly 2.8 times the tonnage, measured across two different rule eras.
    Who operates the Disney Dream?
    Disney Cruise Line. Current status: in service.
    Does the Disney Dream fit through the Panama Canal?
    Not the original locks — but yes through the 2016 Neopanamax locks: at 340 m by 37 m on a 8.1 m draft she clears the 366 × 51.25 × 15.2 m expanded gauge.

    About the data. Every specification is compiled from operator, builder and registry sources and cited per row. Fleet and status figures are snapshots, marked ≈ where approximate and dated; individually verified figures carry a ✓. Ranks never mix units — cruise ships rank by GT, container ships by TEU, tankers by DWT. All silhouettes are original works of ships.fyi, drawn to true scale. Guides may contain clearly marked affiliate links; they never change what we recommend. Corrections and enquiries: business@luck.fyi — we fix errors visibly, not silently.
    © 2026 ships.fyi
    RecordsExplained
